deployment.name: Public geospatial Q&A (example)
deployment.description: Public-facing conversational interface to Earth-observation datasets. A single server-side ReAct agent traverses a 7-stage pipeline (parse_datetime → geocode → search_collections → select_collection → search_items → compute_stats → render_map). Tool responses default to TEMPLATED (short status messages); flip any tool's RETURN SHAPE to freeform to model passing the raw STAC/stats payload into the LLM — the headline rises because the bill now carries each tool's full raw payload (often thousands of tokens) instead of its short templated summary (a few dozen).

anchor_query.example: Visualize gross primary production (GPP) from 'lis-global-da-gpp' over Mendocino County, California, June to November 2020.

_calibration.source: Measured 2026-06-04 against the public-geospatial-qa-agent (https://github.com/ajinkyakulkarni/public-geospatial-qa-agent) running canned-backend tool payloads through real gpt-5.2 standard-tier calls. Corpus run: 50 hand-curated complete queries + 30 messy public-style queries, six cells (templated|freeform × single-turn|per-stage-confirm × gated|nogate), N≥30 per cell, 2,832 total LLM calls.
_calibration.method: Per-tool cap_tokens = tiktoken o200k_base of the agent's templated tool-status strings. result_tokens_avg per tool = tiktoken of the raw canned payload the freeform mode passes back to the model. stac_search anchored on 15 STAC items × 1,230 tok = 18,435 tok measured directly from the canned backend.

per_turn_input.templated: sysprompt 2814 + agent.input 0 + tool sum 119 = 2933 tok/turn (matches default load)
per_turn_input.freeform: sysprompt 2814 + agent.input 0 + tool sum 20926 = 23740 tok/turn (load preset, flip all tools to freeform via global RETURN SHAPE)

_calibration.supersedes: Earlier paper-stated anchors of 3342 (templated) and 22798 (freeform) were estimates; the measured values above are paper-faithful by construction (paper §6 reflects these measured values).

clarification_strategy._doc: Three patterns measured on the public-geospatial-qa-agent corpus run, 2026-06-04. Pre-flight gate dominates per-stage confirm on cost when more than f_naive of public traffic is missing a required field; crossover values below are derived from the measured cells.

none.doc: Every query runs the full cycle. Cost per query = cycle_cost.
none.applies_when: Closed audience that always supplies date + place + dataset.

pre_flight_gate.doc: One small LLM call before the cycle decides 'OK' or asks the user to confirm a proposed default. Cost per query = gate_cost + (1 − f_naive · (1 − recovery_rate)) · cycle_cost.
pre_flight_gate.applies_when: Public-facing traffic where ≥ f_naive of queries lack a required field. Production analog: task-oriented bot slot-filling (Intercom Fin, Klarna's support bot, voice-assistant NLU).

per_stage_confirm.doc: Cycle always runs; confirmations are inline at each input-resolution stage. Each pending stage costs ~1.7× single-turn (templated) or 1.3× (freeform) because the model writes confirmation prose at each pause.
per_stage_confirm.applies_when: Analyst-facing tool where the user expects to confirm at each step (wizard-style UIs).

tunables.f_naive_doc: Share of incoming public traffic missing a required field (date, place, or dataset hint). Set 0 for internal tools; 0.3 for informed-public; 0.5+ for general-public deployments.
tunables.recovery_rate_doc: Share of gated queries whose user comes back and provides the missing field. Production gate UX dependent; 0.9 is a reasonable default.

crossover.doc: Above these f_naive thresholds the pre-flight gate is strictly cheaper than running every query.

set_datetime.label: parse_datetime
set_datetime.description: Validate and normalize a date range to an ISO-8601 interval. Templated response is the parsed interval + confirmation request. Cost: $0 (pure compute, infra-absorbed). If you have a metered date-parser service, enter your per-call rate.

get_place.label: geocode
get_place.description: Resolve a place name to bbox + admin geometry. Templated returns a short confirmation; freeform returns the full GeoJSON polygon. Cost: $0 self-hosted Nominatim/Geodini (infra in the Infrastructure block, e.g. $90/mo line). Managed alternatives: Mapbox ~$0.005/call, Google Geocoding ~$0.005/call. Enter your measured rate above if metered.

collections_rag.label: search_collections
collections_rag.description: Semantic search over the STAC catalog. Templated returns just the count + selection prompt; freeform returns the Top-K collection metadata (descriptions, summaries, asset dictionaries) measured at ~300 tok/collection × 5 collections via the public STAC catalog's /collections endpoint. Cost: $0 self-hosted vector DB (infra-absorbed). Derived: monthly vector-DB infra $ / monthly queries = per-call rate (e.g., $36/mo RAG service / 900K queries ≈ $0.00004/call). Managed: OpenAI File Search $0.0025/call.

select_collection.label: select_collection
select_collection.description: Records the user's collection + variable selection (small either way). Cost: $0 (pure server-side state mutation).

stac_search.label: search_items
stac_search.description: Enumerate catalog items inside the AOI and datetime window. Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the full STAC item array — measured at 15 items × 1,230 tok = 18,435 tok directly from the public STAC catalog's /search endpoint against lis-global-da-gpp over Mendocino County. This is the dominant freeform lever. Cost: $0 self-hosted STAC API (infra-absorbed; the catalog used here is community-hosted). Commercial STAC providers (Planet, Maxar, etc.) typically $0.001–$0.01/call.

stats.label: compute_stats
stats.description: Per-item zonal statistics (mean/median/min/max/percentiles). Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the per-item stats dictionaries (~40 tok/item × 15 items = 600 tok estimated). Cost: $0 self-hosted raster compute (infra-absorbed, e.g., titiler / rio-tiler on your stack). Commercial: COG-stats services $0.0005–$0.002/call depending on resolution.

viz.label: render_map
viz.description: Build a raster tile URL for the selected items + AOI. Small either way — visualization metadata is bounded. Cost: $0 self-hosted titiler / TiTiler-PgSTAC (infra-absorbed). Managed tile services (Mapbox, AWS Location): ~$0.50/1k tile-renders amortized.

agents.label: Geo Q&A Agent (server-side ReAct)
agents.description: Single server-side ReAct agent. Per-call input = sysprompt (2,814 cached) + 0 fresh; tool returns contribute via the registry above. At templated default, total per-turn input = 2,933 tok matching the measured paper anchor.

mixed.label: Mixed (40% full · 30% rag · 15% partial · 10% refusal · 5% heavy)

segments.label: Public visitors
segments.description: Anonymous public visitors. Single-segment simplified — load public-geospatial-qa-multi-segment.json for the paper's 500-auth + 10K-public split (auth contributes ~1.6% of monthly queries).

phases.label: Year 1 — pilot on API
phases.description: Low-risk start. Validate workload + cache hit rate against real usage before committing to spend.

phases.label: Year 2 — committed-spend reservation
phases.description: Lock in a discount with Azure PTU or OpenAI Enterprise commit once volume is steady.

phases.label: Year 3 — hybrid 50/50
phases.description: Stand up Llama-based self-host for steady baseline traffic; keep API for spikes and tail queries.
